text.title: PM Tools Overview Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the PM Tools Overview methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Requirements MUST be classified MoSCoW (Must / Should / Could / Won't); flat requirement lists are rejected.
rule.rationale: without MoSCoW, every requirement is treated equal and the tool selection loses signal.

rule.statement: Each requirement MUST cite at least one anchored pain point (transcript line, retro action, support ticket pattern); aspirational requirements without pain are rejected.
rule.rationale: aspirational requirements pull the selection toward feature-rich expensive tools that nobody uses.

rule.statement: Candidate list MUST be between 3 and 10 tools with explicit reason for inclusion; longer or empty lists are rejected.
rule.rationale: >10 candidates causes paralysis; <3 misses real alternatives.

rule.statement: Each Must requirement MUST be evaluated fit/gap per candidate (fit / partial / gap); blanket 'all good' answers are rejected.
rule.rationale: blanket answers hide the gap that decides the selection.

rule.statement: Shortlist for deeper comparison MUST be 2-3 candidates; longer shortlists defeat the funnel.
rule.rationale: shortlists >3 escalate to scattered PoCs that compare nothing.

rule.statement: Overview MUST name a single decision_owner who will sponsor the comparison phase.
